title: US gov't allows Chinese telecom giant ZTE to purchase Nvidia H200 AI chips — firm joins Alibaba, Tencent, and ByteDance in access to Hopper tech
headline_summary: US government licenses ZTE to purchase restricted Nvidia H200 chips
blurb: The US licensed Chinese telecom ZTE to purchase restricted Nvidia H200 AI chips, joining Alibaba, Tencent, and ByteDance with access to Hopper technology. Chinese regulators and domestic procurement initiatives limit the material impact of this shift in export policy. For VC readers tracking geopolitical tech dynamics, this marks a softening of US restrictions on advanced chip access to major Chinese firms, reshaping AI development capacity in the region.

title: How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product
headline_summary: DeepMind researcher raises $300M pre-seed for visual AI
blurb: Andrew Dai, a former DeepMind researcher, closed a $300M pre-seed funding round before launching any product. Over a decade, Dai built influential AI systems whose research directly informed the development of ChatGPT and other foundational models. The pre-seed valuation signals investor conviction that visual AI represents the next frontier, with institutional capital backing his research track record rather than initial shipping timelines.

title: AWS EC2 and AI leader Dave Brown to exit, replaced by Amazon exec and Microsoft vet Dave Treadwell
headline_summary: AWS EC2 and AI lead Dave Brown exits; Treadwell succeeds
blurb: Dave Brown exits his AWS EC2 and AI leadership role after nearly 19 years with the company. Dave Treadwell, who spent 27 years at Microsoft and is a longtime Amazon executive, assumes the role August 1. Leadership changes at AWS's core infrastructure layer directly impact product roadmaps, pricing, and competitive positioning for all cloud-dependent startups—making this transition worth watching for any VC firm.

title: Yann LeCun launched a new VC fund — then quit. What happened?
headline_summary: Yann LeCun launches VC fund, then quits
blurb: Yann LeCun launched a new VC fund with ambitions in AI-backed startups, then abruptly departed. Meta's chief AI scientist started the fund but exited shortly after, suggesting operational tension or strategic misalignment. The quick departure raises critical questions for VCs about retaining technical AI leaders in venture and whether new AI-focused funds face fundamental structural challenges in attracting committed leadership.

title: Tom Blomfield is taking a leave of absence from YC to join Anthropic
headline_summary: Tom Blomfield leaves Y Combinator to join Anthropic
blurb: Tom Blomfield is taking leave from Y Combinator to join Anthropic. Blomfield founded Wise and spent years at YC; he's now moving to the AI company as it scales toward becoming a core player in frontier AI infrastructure. The move reflects how top venture talent is consolidating around AI's highest-stakes bets, clearly signaling where the best builders see capital flowing next.

title: This founder sold his startup to Klarna for €110m. Now he's raised €12m to build again
headline_summary: Klarna-exit founder raises €12m to build again
blurb: A founder who previously exited to Klarna for €110m is raising €12m to build a new company. The rapid turnaround between exit and fresh capital raise reflects both founder confidence and investor appetite for proven operators with successful acquisition track records. Exits serve as credibility stamps for repeat entrepreneurs; this capital round demonstrates how successful founders cycle back to building.

title: OpenAI power consolidates under co-founder Greg Brockman ahead of prospective IPO
headline_summary: Greg Brockman consolidates OpenAI power as Simo departs
blurb: Greg Brockman consolidates operational authority at OpenAI as Fidji Simo departs due to a chronic medical condition. Simo held a senior product role; his exit reshuffles leadership ahead of OpenAI's expected IPO. C-suite reshuffles at pre-IPO unicorns typically precede going-public processes. This signals governance restructuring and investor positioning: the company and backers believe the executive team can handle public-market scrutiny.

title: Corporate Venture Capital Is Splitting In Two
headline_summary: Corporate venture capital market fractures amid PayPal, Fidelity exits
blurb: PayPal and Fidelity International are winding down their corporate venture capital programs in what looks like sector retreat. But the actual trend is consolidation: power concentrating among elite corporate VCs that can sustain programs long-term. Smaller corporate funds will face mounting pressure as the market splits between well-capitalized tier-one players and underfunded competitors struggling to survive.

title: EDX Markets Closes $76M Series C Led by SBI Holdings
headline_summary: EDX Markets closes $76M Series C led by SBI Holdings
blurb: EDX Markets, an institutional-only crypto trading venue with its own central clearinghouse, closed a $76 million Series C funding round. SBI Holdings, the Tokyo-listed financial group, led the round and became a strategic investor. The funding signals strong institutional appetite for regulated crypto infrastructure and reflects growing mainstream finance acceptance of professional-grade digital asset trading as a core business capability.

title: San Diego-based Self Inspection, which uses AI to assess body damage on a car with as little tech as a smartphone camera, raised $10M led by Sheryl Sandberg (Sean O'Kane/TechCrunch)
headline_summary: Sheryl Sandberg leads $10M into car-damage AI startup Self Inspection
blurb: Self Inspection raised $10 million in a round led by Sheryl Sandberg, with backing from Jon McNeill's DVx Ventures. The San Diego startup automates car damage assessment using AI-powered smartphone images, replacing manual inspections in insurance claims and repair workflows. Automating damage evaluation removes a key friction point in claims processing and vehicle repair operations.

title: Filing: a Chinese company says a fund in which its unit invested paid ~$420M for an indirect 0.8265% stake in DeepSeek, implying DeepSeek is valued at ~$52B (Eduardo Baptista/Reuters)
headline_summary: DeepSeek valued at $52B via Chinese fund stake filing
blurb: DeepSeek, a Chinese AI company, is valued at approximately $52 billion according to a regulatory filing by Anhui Korrun. The Chinese company's subsidiary deployed 2.90 billion yuan ($420 million) to acquire an indirect 0.8265% stake through a fund investment. The valuation positions DeepSeek as a major player in the global AI race and signals China's growing competitiveness in large-language models.

title: Sheryl Sandberg leads $10 million investment in AI-powered vehicle inspection service
headline_summary: Sheryl Sandberg leads $10M AI vehicle inspection startup investment
blurb: Sheryl Sandberg is leading a $10 million investment round in an AI-powered vehicle inspection service startup. The five-year-old company provides enterprise customers with a smartphone-based platform to scan and automatically detect vehicle damage, streamlining fleet management operations at scale. The funding signals sustained investor appetite for AI-driven enterprise applications and reflects growing market demand for automotive fleet inspection and damage assessment automation.

title: Runta, which provides isolated sandboxes and guardrails for AI agents to prevent operational risks, raised a $20M seed led by a16z at a $100M+ valuation (Stephanie Palazzolo/The Information)
headline_summary: Runta raises $20M seed from a16z for AI agent safety
blurb: Runta provides isolated execution sandboxes and safety guardrails that constrain autonomous AI agent behavior to prevent operational damage. a16z led a $20M seed round at $100M+ valuation. The investment signals that safety infrastructure for agents is shifting from research problem to table-stakes infrastructure as enterprises prepare for scaled autonomous deployment.

title: Sable, which is building an AI agent named Aiden that lives on a company's website to run live product demos and more, raised $45M from Sequoia and 8VC (Lily Mae Lazarus/Fortune)
headline_summary: Sable raises $45M from Sequoia, 8VC for AI sales agent
blurb: Sable is building Aiden, an AI agent that lives on company websites to autonomously run product demos and close sales. Sequoia and 8VC backed the company in a $45M round, with Sequoia partner Shaun Maguire leading after witnessing an impressive demo firsthand. The deal shows major VCs backing AI agents for enterprise sales, investing in the belief that autonomous agents will handle complex customer conversations.

title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
headline_summary: Claude leads enterprise agent orchestration, but most deployed 'agents' are chatbots
blurb: Enterprise organizations are consolidating their agent orchestration onto model-provider platforms. Across 101 enterprises surveyed, Anthropic's Claude leads by a wide margin, selected for reliable multi-step execution and model quality. Reality significantly lags ambition: most deployed 'agents' are still chatbot wrappers, and real-time token-burn control remains the exception.

title: Robotics startup Monumental raises $32m backed by Khosla Ventures
headline_summary: Monumental raises $32M Series funding from Khosla Ventures
blurb: Monumental, a robotics startup, closed a $32M Series funding round led by Khosla Ventures, the most active deeptech investor. The investment reflects strong market appetite for hardware automation platforms entering production. Khosla's backing signals sustained conviction in near-term robotics deployment, underscoring the sector's transition from research labs into real-world production environments across industrial and commercial verticals with immediate scaling demand.

title: Exclusive: Aramco in talks to back Humanoid at unicorn valuation
headline_summary: Aramco in talks to back Humanoid at unicorn valuation
blurb: Saudi Aramco is in talks to back robotics startup Humanoid at a unicorn-level valuation. The oil conglomerate's substantial financial interest signals serious corporate appetite for humanoid robot systems and embodied AI development. For VCs, this validates strong sector momentum and demonstrates that strategic megacorps with massive balance sheets are increasingly competing directly for top allocations in robotics and autonomous technologies.

title: An Hour With Our Top AI Agent Cost $13.42. You Can’t Hire Anyone For That.
headline_summary: AI agent works one hour for $13.42—cheaper than hiring anyone
blurb: SaaStr deployed an AI agent as VP of Marketing and measured the economics precisely: one hour of work cost $13.42 and delivered 125 completed actions. No human worker can match that price point; the unit economics are fundamentally different. This labor-cost arbitrage is reshaping how enterprises staff roles and represents the core investment thesis driving capital into AI agent companies.

title: Emergent emerges as the latest AI unicorn after raising $130M in funding
headline_summary: Emergent Labs raises $130M Series C, hits unicorn status
blurb: Emergent Labs closed a $130M Series C round, achieving unicorn status in record time. The round was led by Creaegis and Claypond with participation from Khosla Ventures, marking the startup's third major raise in just 10 months. The rapid succession of funding rounds demonstrates strong investor appetite for AI-powered no-code platforms that empower nontechnical users to build production-grade enterprise software.

title: CXMT close to matching Micron's memory capacity in 2026, research claims — would put China on track to become world's second-largest DRAM producer
headline_summary: CXMT closes gap with Micron; China set for second-largest DRAM production
blurb: CXMT's DRAM production capacity is set to match Micron's levels by 2026, according to recent industry research. This positions China as the world's second-largest DRAM producer, behind only South Korea in production volume. The capacity shift shows China's semiconductor manufacturing capabilities and reshapes global memory chip supply chain dynamics—a key market for venture investors tracking deeptech infrastructure and semiconductor competition.

title: Builder-Deployed Markets Overtake Crypto on Hyperliquid
headline_summary: Builder-deployed markets overtake crypto volume on Hyperliquid
blurb: Hyperliquid's HIP-3 markets for stocks, commodities, and indices first topped native crypto volume on July 8, 2026. Builder-deployed markets have led trading on several days since, though single-name stocks still lag crypto and weekend volumes drop sharply. The shift proves decentralized exchange infrastructure can now handle meaningful volumes of non-crypto assets—a significant expansion of addressable markets for crypto platforms.

title: Mercor is in talks for a $20B valuation
headline_summary: Mercor seeks $20B valuation, doubling October's $10B
blurb: Mercor is in talks for a $20 billion valuation, nearly doubling its October peak of $10 billion. The sharp jump across nine months reflects sustained investor appetite for late-stage companies with strong execution records. For venture investors tracking cap table inflation, the milestone signals the market remains willing to deploy capital at premium multiples despite ongoing uncertainty about sustainable valuations.

title: Lovable is Reportedly in Talks to Raise $300M at a $13.2bn Valuation
headline_summary: Lovable seeks $300M at $13.2B valuation in funding round
blurb: Lovable is reportedly raising $300M at a $13.2B valuation, marking a significant funding milestone for the design-development platform. The platform addresses growing demand for AI-assisted tools in developer and creator workflows. For VCs tracking developer tools and productivity platforms, the round signals sustained institutional confidence in high-end software despite recent market uncertainty.

title: SambaNova hits $11 billion valuation as investors back Nvidia chip challengers
headline_summary: SambaNova hits $11B valuation with General Atlantic backing
blurb: SambaNova closed fresh funding at an $11 billion valuation in a round led by General Atlantic, the prominent growth equity firm. The startup designs and manufactures semiconductor accelerators for AI training and inference workloads, positioning itself as a direct alternative to Nvidia in the increasingly competitive accelerator market. Investor backing signals that surging demand for non-Nvidia silicon is substantially outpacing current supply, creating market openings and fragmentation across the critical AI infrastructure stack.

title: PayPal joins the European Payments Council
headline_summary: PayPal joins European Payments Council
blurb: PayPal joined the European Payments Council to shape standards and regulation in European payments infrastructure. The membership reflects the company's commitment to European market governance as payment frameworks and regulatory requirements evolve across the continent. VCs tracking European fintech should note that council participation signals competitive positioning and influence in the systems that structure market access.

title: Grubhub parent Wonder raised a $650M+ Series D at a $9B valuation, bringing its total raised to $3B+ since its founding in 2018, and plans to go public in 2027 (Lily Mae Lazarus/Fortune)
headline_summary: Wonder raises $650M Series D at $9B valuation, eyes 2027 IPO
blurb: Wonder raised $650 million in a Series D round at a $9 billion valuation. The Grubhub parent company, led by Marc Lore, has now accumulated over $3 billion in total capital since 2018 and is targeting a 2027 IPO. For the VC market, this late-stage win demonstrates sustained investor appetite for food delivery and marketplace consolidation despite macro headwinds.

title: Why Greylock capped its new fund at $1.5B when it says it could have raised more
headline_summary: Greylock caps new fund at $1.5B to stay founder-focused
blurb: Greylock Partners capped its latest fund at $1.5B despite having clear capacity to raise more capital from LPs. The firm will maintain roughly 25 investments per fund, intentionally constraining portfolio size versus historical patterns. This self-imposed cap signals a strategic pivot: the firm believes concentrated, hands-on founder partnerships and deeper engagement drive superior returns compared to chasing maximum assets under management.

title: Stripe’s Acquisition Pace Has Accelerated In The Past Five Years, But Nothing Comes Close To Its Reported $53B PayPal Bet
headline_summary: Stripe, Advent International bid $53B for troubled PayPal
blurb: Stripe and Advent International have made an offer to acquire PayPal for $53 billion. The deal values the troubled payments giant at a steep discount from its 2021 peak, marking significant consolidation in the fintech infrastructure layer. For VCs, this is a watershed signal: even mature, once-dominant payment processors are now acquisition targets at valuations well below their private-market highs.

title: OpenAI’s Growing Challenges Narrow Its IPO Window
headline_summary: OpenAI's IPO window narrows amid mounting challenges
blurb: OpenAI's IPO window is narrowing as operational and market challenges mount. The company faces a compressed timeline due to regulatory scrutiny, competitive pressure, and execution hurdles affecting investor confidence and public-market readiness. For VCs managing AI unicorn exits, this is instructive: timing windows close faster than most plan, and external conditions reshape when category-leaders can go public.
